title: EEG study of the attentional blink; before, during, and after transcranial Direct Current Stimulation (tDCS)
description: This dataset comprises concurrent EEG and transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) recordings from 40 participants performing an attentional blink task. Participants completed two laboratory sessions approximately one week apart, receiving either anodal or cathodal tDCS (1 mA, 20 minutes) while EEG was recorded before, during, and after stimulation. The dataset documents the physiological effects of tDCS on neural correlates of attention and provides a resource for investigating artifact characteristics in concurrent tDCS-EEG recordings.
methods_description: EEG was recorded during an attentional blink task in three blocks per visit (pre-tDCS, during tDCS, post-tDCS), each approximately 20 minutes long. tDCS was applied at 1 mA intensity for 20 minutes with 1-minute ramp-up and ramp-down periods, using a 7×5 mm electrode montage centered over F3 (anode or cathode) and right forehead/Fp2 (cathode or anode). Two visits were conducted approximately one week apart with counterbalanced anodal and cathodal stimulation conditions.
